zh.Loughborough, Leicestershire, England: 英國萊希斯特斯爾洛賀波洛
zh.Willoughby on the Wolds, Nottinghamshire: 英國諾廷加姆爾維洛賀比 奧 馮 沃爾茲
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Willoughby V於大約1260年出生在英國諾廷加姆爾維洛賀比 奧 馮 沃爾茲時，他的父親Richard de Willoughby31歲，母親Sibille of Nottinghamshire26歲。他與Elizabeth de Vautort結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1324年在英國德比夏爾利斯利享年65歲，埋葬在英國諾廷加姆爾維洛賀比 奧 馮 沃爾茲。

zh-hans.Willoughby on the Wolds, Nottinghamshire: 英国諾廷加姆爾維洛賀比 奧 馮 沃爾茲
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard de Willoughby V于大约1260年出生在英国諾廷加姆爾維洛賀比 奧 馮 沃爾茲时，他的父亲Richard de Willoughby31岁，母亲Sibille of Nottinghamshire26岁。他与Elizabeth de Vautort结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1324年在英国德比夏爾利斯利享年65岁，埋葬在英国諾廷加姆爾維洛賀比 奧 馮 沃爾茲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Willoughby
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Willoughby, for example in Leicestershire, Lincolnshire, Nottinghamshire, and Warwickshire. They are named from an Old English +wilig ‘willow’ + Old Norse bȳ ‘farmstead, village’.

variants.name: Wilby
meanings: habitational name from any of the places called Wilby, in Suffolk, Norfolk, and Northamptonshire. The first placename is probably named from an Old English wilig ‘willow’ + Old English bēag ‘circle, ring’; the Norfolk placename derives from Old English wilig + bēag or Old Norse bý ‘farmstead, village’; the Northamptonshire placename derives from the Old Norse personal name Vili + bý.
Redmonds has demonstrated that in the parish of Hipperholme, and subsequently elsewhere in Yorkshire, Wilby occurs as a variant of Willoughby .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Willoughby V was born about 1260, in Willoughby on the Wolds, Nottinghamshire, England, his father, Richard de Willoughby, was 31 and his mother, Sibille of Nottinghamshire, was 26. He married Elizabeth de Vautort. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1324, in Risley, Derbyshire, England, at the age of 65, and was buried in Willoughby on the Wolds, Nottinghamshire, England.

fr.Willoughby on the Wolds, Nottinghamshire: Willoughby on the Wolds, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Willoughby V est né environ 1260, à Willoughby on the Wolds, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard de Willoughby, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Sibille of Nottinghamshire, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth de Vautort. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1324, à Risley, Derbyshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterré à Willoughby on the Wolds, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Willoughby Vが約1260年にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアウィロウビー オン ス ウオルズで生まれた時，父Richard de Willoughbyは31歳，母Sibille of Nottinghamshireは26歳であった。Elizabeth de Vautortと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1324年にイギリスデルブイシュアイアリスリーで65歳で死去し，イギリスノッティンガムシュアイアウィロウビー オン ス ウオルズにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Willoughby V(이)가 대략 1260년 영국 노틴그함실르 윌루그h비  테 울드즈에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard de Willoughby(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Sibille of Nottinghamshire(은)는 26세였다. 그는 Elizabeth de Vautort(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1324년 영국 더비실르 리슬리에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노틴그함실르 윌루그h비  테 울드즈에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Elizabeth de Vautort. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1324, em Risley, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, com 65 anos, e foi sepultado em Willoughby on the Wolds, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra.

ru.Willoughby on the Wolds, Nottinghamshire: уиллоугби он т волдс, ноттингхамшир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Willoughby V родился около 1260 г. в местности уиллоугби он т волдс, ноттингхамшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard de Willoughby было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Sibille of Nottinghamshire, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth de Vautort. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1324 г. в местности рисли, дирбишир, Англия в возрасте 65 лет и был похоронен в местности уиллоугби он т волдс, ноттингхамшир, Англия.
